title: High Thermal Conductivity and Radiative Cooling Designed Boron Nitride Nanosheets/Silk Fibroin Films for Personal Thermal Management
subtitle: ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 2024

slides.title: High Thermal Conductivity and Radiative Cooling Designed Boron Nitride Nanosheets/Silk Fibroin Films for Personal Thermal Management
slides.subtitle: ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 2024
slides.note: ACS abstract, article page, figures 1-4 and conclusion.

cards.title: Why it matters
cards.text: Cooling is energy intensive, so passive skin-contact cooling is a practical target.

cards.title: Main bottleneck
cards.text: Pure silk is biocompatible but not conductive enough for rapid heat dissipation.

cards.title: Core idea
cards.text: Use BNNS as a directional heat spreader and silk fibroin as a durable matrix.

items.title: Silk thermal transport
items.desc: Silk becomes a serious thermal-material candidate.

items.title: BNNS composite films
items.desc: Oriented BN starts to dominate in-plane heat flow.

items.title: Wearable passive cooling
items.desc: Textiles and films converge for body cooling.

items.title: BNNS/SF film
items.desc: The paper integrates alignment, reflectance and biocompatibility.

slides.title: Benchmark 对比
rows: High filler loading may reduce softness

steps.title: Prepare BNNS
steps.desc: Exfoliate h-BN into nanosheets without losing the platelet structure.

steps.title: Mix with SF
steps.desc: Combine with silk fibroin before vacuum filtration.

steps.title: Build alignment
steps.desc: Layer-by-layer stacking forms a directional thermal path.

steps.title: Validate
steps.desc: Measure thermal conductivity, reflectance, emissivity and biosafety.

slides.title: 核心公式与机理
slides.formula: λ = α × ρ × Cp; TCE = (λcomposite - λSF) / λSF × 100%

cards.title: Method validation
cards.text: Structure, thermal and cooling behavior are all checked.

cards.title: Indoor tests
cards.text: Simulated skin and wearable scenarios are used.

cards.title: Outdoor tests
cards.text: Sunlight or environment are incorporated when relevant.

cards.title: Stability
cards.text: Cycle, wetting or degradation are quantified.

cards.title: Heat transfer
cards.text: The filler network changes phonon pathways and heat spreading.

cards.title: Interface control
cards.text: Surface chemistry decides whether the network remains useful.

cards.title: Skin contact
cards.text: Wearable patches and body surface cooling.

cards.title: Bioelectronics
cards.text: Heat removal from LEDs or soft devices.

cards.title: Reusable settings
cards.text: Washing, cycling or reuse depending on the paper.

slides.title: 局限与展望
limitations: Very high filler loading may reduce softness.
Large-area alignment is harder to scale.
Wear, sweat and washing need broader field testing.
futureWork: Optimize interfacial chemistry to reduce phonon scattering.
Develop roll-to-roll or other scalable alignment routes.
Test laundering and fatigue under realistic clothing conditions.
Co-design with textile architectures for all-day comfort.

items.text: BNNS and silk fibroin are combined into an aligned, reusable cooling film that balances heat spreading, radiative cooling and skin compatibility.
The main design principle is to align filler geometry, interfacial chemistry and matrix choice with the target cooling mode.
